short_post: Blood clotting disorders have been linked to COVID-19 vaccines, according to a 2025 National Academies report. However, we still lack key details about how often this happens or which groups face the highest risk. {{ARTICLE_URL}} #BasedOnScience
long_post: You may have heard concerns about blood clots after COVID-19 vaccines. Here's what the science tells us: According to a 2025 National Academies report, blood clotting disorders have indeed been linked to COVID-19 vaccines, and the evidence for this connection has grown stronger over time. The report also notes cases of acute myocarditis following mRNA vaccines. However, major data gaps remain - we don't know exactly how common these side effects are or which populations face the highest risk. Scientists across 8 countries are working together to study background rates of these health problems to better understand what's normal versus vaccine-related. Talk to your doctor about your personal risk factors. {{ARTICLE_URL}} #BasedOnScience
